title: nlink-lab status --json <LAB> output
description: Emitted by `nlink-lab status --json <LAB>` (the per-lab variant — different from the no-arg list and the --scan summary). Wraps the lab's serialised topology with two augmentations: per-node `addresses` map (resolved IPs incl. mgmt0) and a `host_resources` block describing the host-side artefacts the lab installed. The topology fields themselves are not schematised here — refer to the lib's Topology type. Round-5 §1.2.

lab.description: Lab metadata (name, description, tags, etc.). Shape mirrors `LabConfig` in the library.

nodes.description: Per-node details. Each node value carries the topology Node fields plus an `addresses` map (interface name → list of CIDR strings) when at least one address resolved. Schema is open-ended; consumers that need stability for specific fields should pin to the library type.

addresses.description: Resolved IPs per interface. Includes `mgmt0` for host-reachable mgmt setups.

links.description: Topology links — see the library's Link type for fields.

networks.description: Bridge networks — see the library's Network type for fields.

host_resources.description: Host-side artefacts the lab installed. Lets consumers detect cross-lab collisions client-side without netlink.

mgmt_bridge.description: Mgmt bridge interface name in the host network namespace (`nl{hash8}`). Always present for namespace-only labs.

subnets.description: Every declared subnet in the topology (links + networks), normalised to network-address/prefix form. Useful to verify two parallel labs don't claim the same private range.
